Clusters.description: A list of the Clusters that exist in your AWS account.

NextToken.description: Token for the next result.

Networks.description: An array of networks that you have created.

Versions.description: List of engine versions that are available for this AWS account.

NextToken.description: Placeholder documentation for __string

Arn.description: The unique arn of the channel.

CdiInputSpecification.description: Specification of CDI inputs for this channel

ChannelClass.description: The class for this channel. STANDARD for a channel with two pipelines or SINGLE_PIPELINE for a channel with one pipeline.

Destinations.description:
A list of destinations of the channel. For UDP outputs, there is one
destination per output. For other types (HLS, for example), there is
one destination per packager.

EgressEndpoints.description: The endpoints where outgoing connections initiate from

Id.description: The unique id of the channel.

InputAttachments.description: List of input attachments for channel.

InputSpecification.description: Specification of network and file inputs for this channel

LogLevel.description: The log level being written to CloudWatch Logs.

PipelineDetails.description: Runtime details for the pipelines of a running channel.

PipelinesRunningCount.description: The number of currently healthy pipelines.

RoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role assumed when running the Channel.

ChannelEngineVersion.description: Requested engine version for this channel.

Arn.description: The Unique ARN of the input (generated, immutable).

AttachedChannels.description: A list of channel IDs that that input is attached to (currently an input can only be attached to one channel).

Destinations.description: A list of the destinations of the input (PUSH-type).

Id.description: The generated ID of the input (unique for user account, immutable).

InputClass.description:
STANDARD - MediaLive expects two sources to be connected to this input. If the channel is also STANDARD, both sources will be ingested. If the channel is SINGLE_PIPELINE, only the first source will be ingested; the second source will always be ignored, even if the first source fails.
SINGLE_PIPELINE - You can connect only one source to this input. If the ChannelClass is also SINGLE_PIPELINE, this value is valid. If the ChannelClass is STANDARD, this value is not valid because the channel requires two sources in the input.

InputPartnerIds.description: A list of IDs for all Inputs which are partners of this one.

InputSourceType.description:
Certain pull input sources can be dynamic, meaning that they can have their URL's dynamically changes
during input switch actions. Presently, this functionality only works with MP4_FILE and TS_FILE inputs.

MediaConnectFlows.description: A list of MediaConnect Flows for this input.

Name.description: The user-assigned name (This is a mutable value).

RoleArn.description: The Amazon Resource Name (ARN) of the role this input assumes during and after creation.

SecurityGroups.description: A list of IDs for all the Input Security Groups attached to the input.

Sources.description: A list of the sources of the input (PULL-type).

Type.description: The different types of inputs that AWS Elemental MediaLive supports.

SrtSettings.description: The settings associated with an SRT input.

InputNetworkLocation.description:
The location of this input. AWS, for an input existing in the AWS Cloud, On-Prem for
an input in a customer network.

Smpte2110ReceiverGroupSettings.description: Include this parameter if the input is a SMPTE 2110 input, to identify the stream sources for this input.

RouterSettings.description: Information about any MediaConnect router association with this input.

Arn.description: The unique ARN of the input device.

ConnectionState.description: The state of the connection between the input device and AWS.

DeviceSettingsSyncState.description: The status of the action to synchronize the device configuration. If you change the configuration of the input device (for example, the maximum bitrate), MediaLive sends the new data to the device. The device might not update itself immediately. SYNCED means the device has updated its configuration. SYNCING means that it has not updated its configuration.

DeviceUpdateStatus.description: The status of software on the input device.

HdDeviceSettings.description: Settings that describe an input device that is type HD.

Id.description: The unique ID of the input device.

MacAddress.description: The network MAC address of the input device.

Name.description: A name that you specify for the input device.

NetworkSettings.description: The network settings for the input device.

SerialNumber.description: The unique serial number of the input device.

Type.description: The type of the input device.

UhdDeviceSettings.description: Settings that describe an input device that is type UHD.

AvailabilityZone.description: The Availability Zone associated with this input device.

MedialiveInputArns.description: An array of the ARNs for the MediaLive inputs attached to the device. Returned only if the outputType is MEDIALIVE_INPUT.

OutputType.description: The output attachment type of the input device. Specifies MEDIACONNECT_FLOW if this device is the source for a MediaConnect flow. Specifies MEDIALIVE_INPUT if this device is the source for a MediaLive input.

Body.description: The binary data for the thumbnail that the Link device has most recently sent to MediaLive.

ContentType.description: Specifies the media type of the thumbnail.

ETag.description: The unique, cacheable version of this thumbnail.

LastModified.description: The date and time the thumbnail was last updated at the device.

Arn.description: Unique ARN of Input Security Group

Id.description: The Id of the Input Security Group

Inputs.description: The list of inputs currently using this Input Security Group.

State.description: The current state of the Input Security Group.

WhitelistRules.description: Whitelist rules and their sync status

Arn.description: The unique arn of the multiplex.

AvailabilityZones.description: A list of availability zones for the multiplex.

Destinations.description: A list of the multiplex output destinations.

Id.description: The unique id of the multiplex.

PipelinesRunningCount.description: The number of currently healthy pipelines.

ProgramCount.description: The number of programs in the multiplex.

State.description: The current state of the multiplex.

ChannelId.description: The MediaLive channel associated with the program.

MultiplexProgramSettings.description: The settings for this multiplex program.

PacketIdentifiersMap.description: The packet identifier map for this multiplex program.

PipelineDetails.description: Contains information about the current sources for the specified program in the specified multiplex. Keep in mind that each multiplex pipeline connects to both pipelines in a given source channel (the channel identified by the program). But only one of those channel pipelines is ever active at one time.

ProgramName.description: The name of the multiplex program.

CurrencyCode.description: Currency code for usagePrice and fixedPrice in ISO-4217 format, e.g. 'USD'

FixedPrice.description: One-time charge for each reserved resource, e.g. '0.0' for a NO_UPFRONT offering

OfferingDescription.description: Offering description, e.g. 'HD AVC output at 10-20 Mbps, 30 fps, and standard VQ in US West (Oregon)'

UsagePrice.description: Recurring usage charge for each reserved resource, e.g. '157.0'

CurrencyCode.description: Currency code for usagePrice and fixedPrice in ISO-4217 format, e.g. 'USD'

End.description: Reservation UTC end date and time in ISO-8601 format, e.g. '2019-03-01T00:00:00'

FixedPrice.description: One-time charge for each reserved resource, e.g. '0.0' for a NO_UPFRONT offering

OfferingDescription.description: Offering description, e.g. 'HD AVC output at 10-20 Mbps, 30 fps, and standard VQ in US West (Oregon)'

Start.description: Reservation UTC start date and time in ISO-8601 format, e.g. '2018-03-01T00:00:00'

UsagePrice.description: Recurring usage charge for each reserved resource, e.g. '157.0'

NextToken.description: The next token; for use in pagination.

ScheduleActions.description: The list of actions in the schedule.

InputDeviceTransfers.description: The list of devices that you are transferring or are being transferred to you.

NextToken.description: A token to get additional list results.
Token for the next ListMultiplexProgram request.

Arn.description: A cloudwatch alarm template's ARN (Amazon Resource Name)

ComparisonOperator.description: The comparison operator used to compare the specified statistic and the threshold.

DatapointsToAlarm.description: The number of datapoints within the evaluation period that must be breaching to trigger the alarm.

EvaluationPeriods.description: The number of periods over which data is compared to the specified threshold.

GroupId.description: A cloudwatch alarm template group's id. AWS provided template groups have ids that start with `aws-`

Id.description: A cloudwatch alarm template's id. AWS provided templates have ids that start with `aws-`

MetricName.description: The name of the metric associated with the alarm. Must be compatible with targetResourceType.

Name.description: A resource's name. Names must be unique within the scope of a resource type in a specific region.

Period.description: The period, in seconds, over which the specified statistic is applied.

Statistic.description: The statistic to apply to the alarm's metric data.

Tags.description: Represents the tags associated with a resource.

TargetResourceType.description: The resource type this template should dynamically generate cloudwatch metric alarms for.

Threshold.description: The threshold value to compare with the specified statistic.

TreatMissingData.description: Specifies how missing data points are treated when evaluating the alarm's condition.

Arn.description: A cloudwatch alarm template group's ARN (Amazon Resource Name)

Id.description: A cloudwatch alarm template group's id. AWS provided template groups have ids that start with `aws-`

Name.description: A resource's name. Names must be unique within the scope of a resource type in a specific region.

Tags.description: Represents the tags associated with a resource.

Arn.description: An eventbridge rule template's ARN (Amazon Resource Name)

EventType.description: The type of event to match with the rule.

GroupId.description: An eventbridge rule template group's id. AWS provided template groups have ids that start with `aws-`

Id.description: An eventbridge rule template's id. AWS provided templates have ids that start with `aws-`

Name.description: A resource's name. Names must be unique within the scope of a resource type in a specific region.

Tags.description: Represents the tags associated with a resource.

Arn.description: An eventbridge rule template group's ARN (Amazon Resource Name)

Id.description: An eventbridge rule template group's id. AWS provided template groups have ids that start with `aws-`

Name.description: A resource's name. Names must be unique within the scope of a resource type in a specific region.

Tags.description: Represents the tags associated with a resource.

Arn.description: A signal map's ARN (Amazon Resource Name)

DiscoveryEntryPointArn.description: A top-level supported AWS resource ARN to discovery a signal map from.

ErrorMessage.description: Error message associated with a failed creation or failed update attempt of a signal map.

FailedMediaResourceMap.description: A map representing an incomplete AWS media workflow as a graph.

LastSuccessfulMonitorDeployment.description: Represents the latest successful monitor deployment of a signal map.

MediaResourceMap.description: A map representing an AWS media workflow as a graph.

MonitorChangesPendingDeployment.description: If true, there are pending monitor changes for this signal map that can be deployed.

MonitorDeployment.description: Represents the latest monitor deployment of a signal map.

Name.description: A resource's name. Names must be unique within the scope of a resource type in a specific region.

Status.description: A signal map's current status which is dependent on its lifecycle actions or associated jobs.

Tags.description: Represents the tags associated with a resource.

Arn.description: The ARN of this ChannelPlacementGroup. It is automatically assigned when the ChannelPlacementGroup is created.

ClusterId.description: The ID of the Cluster that the Node belongs to.

Id.description: The ID of the ChannelPlacementGroup. Unique in the AWS account. The ID is the resource-id portion of the ARN.

Name.description: The name that you specified for the ChannelPlacementGroup.

Nodes.description: An array with one item, which is the single Node that is associated with the ChannelPlacementGroup.

State.description: The current state of the ChannelPlacementGroup.

Arn.description: The ARN of this Cluster. It is automatically assigned when the Cluster is created.

ClusterType.description: The hardware type for the Cluster

Id.description: The ID of the  Cluster. Unique in the AWS account. The ID is the resource-id portion of the ARN.

InstanceRoleArn.description: The ARN of the IAM role for the Node in this Cluster. Any Nodes that are associated with this Cluster assume this role. The role gives permissions to the operations that you expect these Node to perform.

Name.description: The name that you specified for the Cluster.

NetworkSettings.description: Network settings that connect the Nodes in the Cluster to one or more of the Networks that the Cluster is associated with.

State.description: The current state of the Cluster.

Arn.description: The ARN of this Network. It is automatically assigned when the Network is created.

Id.description: The ID of the Network. Unique in the AWS account. The ID is the resource-id portion of the ARN.

IpPools.description: An array of IpPools in your organization's network that identify a collection of IP addresses in this network that are reserved for use in MediaLive Anywhere. MediaLive Anywhere uses these IP addresses for Push inputs (in both Bridge and NAT networks) and for output destinations (only in Bridge networks). Each IpPool specifies one CIDR block.

Name.description: The name that you specified for the Network.

Routes.description: An array of routes that MediaLive Anywhere needs to know about in order to route encoding traffic.

State.description: The current state of the Network. Only MediaLive Anywhere can change the state.

Arn.description: The ARN of the Node. It is automatically assigned when the Node is created.

ChannelPlacementGroups.description: An array of IDs. Each ID is one ChannelPlacementGroup that is associated with this Node. Empty if the Node is not yet associated with any groups.

ClusterId.description: The ID of the Cluster that the Node belongs to.

ConnectionState.description: The current connection state of the Node.

Id.description: The unique ID of the Node. Unique in the Cluster. The ID is the resource-id portion of the ARN.

InstanceArn.description: The ARN of the EC2 instance hosting the Node.

Name.description: The name that you specified for the Node.

Role.description: The initial role current role of the Node in the Cluster. ACTIVE means the Node is available for encoding. BACKUP means the Node is a redundant Node and might get used if an ACTIVE Node fails.

State.description: The current state of the Node.

SdiSourceMappings.description: An array of SDI source mappings. Each mapping connects one logical SdiSource to the physical SDI card and port that the physical SDI source uses.

ChannelPlacementGroups.description: An array of ChannelPlacementGroups that exist in the Cluster.

NextToken.description: Token for the next result.
Token for the next result.

Nodes.description: An array of Nodes that exist in the Cluster.

Alerts.description: The alerts found for this channel
